name: Tarte Tatin (a 'Reversed' Apple Pie)
recipeIngredient: 4 3/8 lb, peeled, cored, cut into halves
recipeInstructions:
1. For a 26 cm (10.2 in) diameter pie pan, roll out the pastry to a 30 cm (11.8 in) disc and let it rest in the refrigerator.
2. Preheat the oven to 220°C (435°F).
3. Butter a 26-cm-diameter baking parchment disc and carefully line the base of the tin with it. Spread sugar evenly over the paper, then place in the middle of the oven and bake for about 8 minutes to caramelize.
4. Remove from the oven and add butter, allowing it to melt. Return the pan to the oven briefly.
5. Place the halved apples vertically side by side (higher than the rim of the tin) and return to the oven. Bake for about 25 minutes.
6. Carefully remove the pie from the oven and cover the apples with the pastry disc. Return to the oven for an additional 20-25 minutes, until golden brown.
7. Let cool for 5 minutes on a rack. Turn off the oven.
8. Remove the tin, carefully peel away the parchment, and turn over the tart onto a plate. Serve warm or lukewarm with crème chantilly.
